title: Creamy Peanut Butter Oats
description: A high-calorie, energy-packed breakfast ready in minutes. Great for quick mornings.
explanation: This is a dense breakfast for busy mornings, higher-calorie needs, or pre-planned snacks. Oats and chia make it filling, peanut butter adds richness, and plant milk helps with creaminess.
You can cook it as porridge or prepare it as overnight oats. The base is flexible as long as you keep enough liquid and give the oats time to soften.
instructions: In a small pot, bring the plant milk to a gentle boil.
Add rolled oats and chia seeds. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally until thick.
Stir in the peanut butter and jaggery until well combined and creamy.
Pour into a bowl and top with sliced bananas.
stepNotes: Soy milk gives more protein; almond milk is lighter. Use water plus a splash of plant milk if you want it less rich.
Chia thickens the oats as it sits. Add extra liquid if the pot becomes too thick.
Add peanut butter after the oats soften so it blends smoothly instead of sticking to the pan.
Banana adds sweetness and calories. Use apple, dates, raisins, or berries if banana is not available.
If making overnight oats, chill after mixing and eat cold or warmed the next morning.

variationSections.title: Adjust calories and protein
items: For fewer calories, use 1 tablespoon peanut butter and skip jaggery.
For more protein, use soy milk and add a scoop of plant protein after cooking.
For more calories, add extra peanut butter, dates, nuts, or a second banana.

variationSections.title: Flavor variations
items: Chocolate: add cocoa powder and a little jaggery or dates.
Masala chai: simmer the milk with cardamom, cinnamon, and ginger before adding oats.
Fruit bowl: use apple, mango, berries, or seasonal fruit instead of banana.
